title: Federal Reserve Bank of New York Policy on Counterparties for Market Operations

source.name: Federal Reserve Bank of New York

summary: The Federal Reserve Bank of New York has published its operating policy governing relationships with private sector counterparties for market operations. The policy outlines that the NY Fed transacts primarily with regulated banks and broker-dealers for open market operations conducted under FOMC direction. The document establishes eligibility criteria, operational expectations, and compliance standards that counterparties must meet, including adherence to TMPG best practices and FX Global Code standards.
key_points: NY Fed executes open market operations at FOMC direction under Section 14 of the Federal Reserve Act
Primary counterparties are regulated banks and broker-dealers; other entity types considered only when appropriate
Counterparties must meet minimum capital standards set by their primary regulator
Counterparties must comply with Treasury Market Practices Group (TMPG) best practices and FX Global Code
NY Fed maintains multiple counterparty categories: primary dealers, standing repo, reverse repo, FX, and foreign reserves management counterparties
Participation in NY Fed operations is at the NY Fed's sole discretion and creates no rights for counterparties
why_it_matters: This policy document describes the foundational counterparty infrastructure through which the Federal Reserve conducts monetary policy implementation in markets. Understanding which institutions are eligible to participate in Fed operations provides context for market structure, particularly in repo markets, Treasury markets, and FX markets where the NY Fed actively operates.
macro_context: This document establishes the framework for how the Federal Reserve Bank of New York selects and manages counterparties for open market operations executed under Section 14 of the Federal Reserve Act at FOMC direction. It covers multiple counterparty categories (primary dealers, standing repo, reverse repo, FX, and foreign reserves management) and sets baseline eligibility standards including regulatory capital requirements and industry best practice compliance.
market_structure_context: This document describes the counterparty framework for Fed market operations, including primary dealers (who participate in Treasury auctions and market-making), repo counterparties, and FX counterparties. This framework is foundational to market structure as it defines which institutions can access Federal Reserve operations and execute trades on behalf of the central bank. The document references the Treasury Market Practices Group (TMPG) and FX Global Code as compliance benchmarks, indicating industry-standard best practices that govern participant behavior in these markets.

issues_found: Low informational density for research purposes - document is procedural/policy in nature rather than containing market-moving information
No specific numbers, dates, forecasts, or data points in the source
The document serves as reference material for understanding Fed operational infrastructure rather than providing actionable research content

notes: Low-value source for financial research archive. This is an institutional policy document that outlines the NY Fed's counterparty eligibility criteria and operational expectations. While it touches on market structure elements (counterparty categories, participation standards), it contains no specific numbers, no actionable market information, and no macro catalysts. The document serves as a reference for understanding Fed operational infrastructure rather than providing information that would influence market decisions or risk assessments. Source quality is high (Federal Reserve), but informational content is thin for research purposes.
